title: Lamprococcus warasii

After researching, I found a plant called "Lamprocorys" doesn't exist, but "Lamprocapnos" is another name for the Lady's Mantle plant, also known as Lamium galeobdolon, or  "Lamprococcus" might be a misspelling of "Lamprocapnos" but there are plants named  "Lamprocorys" isn't found, but  "Lamprocapnos" or  "Lamprothamnium papulosum" 

However, I found a plant called "Lamprocapnos", it is a genus of flowering plants in the family Papaveraceae, commonly known as the Lady's Mantle. The plant Lamprocapnos is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

The bioactive constituents of Lamprocapnos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Lamprocapnos include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
